linting.notes: Linting is enforced via pre-commit hooks. The .pre-commit-config.yaml configures ruff (with --fix), ruff-format, and several other checks (merge conflicts, trailing whitespace, large files, end-of-file, toml syntax, uv-lock, uv-export). Excludes .ipynb files from ruff checks.

testing.notes: NO UNIT TESTS EXIST. The repository has no test files, no pytest configuration, and no testing framework. Only an integration test in CI (test-external-providers.yml) that verifies the llama-stack server starts and loads the InstructLab KFT provider.

gating_checks.name: Integration Test - Server Startup
gating_checks.command: uv sync && uv pip install -e . && uv run llama stack run run.yaml --image-type venv

ci.notes: All three workflows run on pull_request and push to main. Pre-commit workflow uses Python 3.11. Test workflow uses Python 3.10 and uv package manager. Build workflow builds multi-arch images (amd64, arm64) and publishes to quay.io on push to main. The integration test waits for server health endpoint and validates provider loading via log grep.

build.notes: Uses uv as the package manager (modern Python package tool). Dockerfile uses multi-stage build with UBI9 Python 3.11, building a wheel in the builder stage and installing it in the runtime stage. The project requires Python >=3.10 and has optional dependencies under 'utils' for CLI tooling (click, instructlab-training).

conventions.notes: Source code is in src/llama_stack_provider_kft/. Main modules are kft_adapter.py (15KB, core adapter logic), provider.py, config.py, and __init__.py. Utility scripts in utils/ include ilab_kft.py CLI, data_upload.py, and cluster_setup.py. Pre-commit hooks enforce code style (ruff), trailing whitespace removal, end-of-file fixing, and uv.lock consistency.

lint_commands.notes: All hooks passed: check-merge-conflict, trailing-whitespace, check-added-large-files, end-of-file-fixer, check-toml, ruff, ruff-format, uv-lock, uv-export

container_recipe.notes: Container starts successfully with repo mounted at /app. Dependencies install cleanly with uv. Linting works perfectly. NO TESTS to run. The llama-stack server can be started for integration testing but requires Kubernetes cluster access for actual training operations.

results.notes: Server starts successfully and loads configuration (timed out after 10s as expected)

validation.summary: install OK, lint OK (no issues), format OK, pre-commit OK, server starts OK. NO UNIT TESTS.

gaps: No unit tests - the repository has zero test files and no testing framework configured
No pytest or other test runner
Only integration test validates server startup, not actual training functionality
Integration tests require Kubernetes cluster infrastructure to test real training jobs
No mock tests for Kubernetes API interactions
No documentation on how to run local development testing
